title: bulbophyllum cheiri subsp. cheiri
intro: Bulbophyllum cheiri subsp. cheiri, also known as the orchid tree or fragrant tree orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family and is native to the tropical forests of Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties that help relieve congestion and coughs. The health benefits of Bulbophyllum cheiri subsp. cheiri include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties that help combat infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
